steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a textual evidence question. The claim to illustrate is that Alexandra Bergson takes comfort in understanding the world around her. The correct quotation must show her finding comfort specifically through grasping order or structure in nature — not just appreciating nature or facing hardship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘She looked fixedly up the bleak street…’: shows Alexandra steeling herself to face a difficult situation with determination, not taking comfort through understanding. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘She had never known before how much the country meant to her…’: expresses how deeply Alexandra values the land, but not that she finds comfort by understanding the world around her. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Alexandra drove off alone…’: describes a solitary nighttime drive; it conveys no emotion about understanding or comfort. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘She always loved to watch them, to think of their vastness and distance, and of their ordered march. It fortified her to reflect upon the great operations of nature, and when she thought of the law that lay behind them, she felt a sense of personal security.’: directly shows Alexandra feeling fortified and secure by reflecting on the law and order governing the stars — understanding the world around her brings comfort. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
